title: Digital twin: The living blueprint | Siemens
body: Digital twin: The living blueprint Move beyond static models with digital twin technology providing a continuous, real-time feedback loop, powered by AI to predict the future, optimize the present, and automate the path forward. Accelerate your digital transformation Siemens and NVIDIA strategic partnership Focusing on industrial AI, digital twins, and the industrial metaverse to bring AI to the physical world. Learn more AI-powered digital twins AI-powered digital twins combine physics-based simulation with real-time operational data to create dynamic, evolving representations of products, machines, and entire production systems. By linking the virtual and physical worlds in a continuous feedback loop, they enable companies to design, validate, and optimize with far greater speed, confidence, and efficiency. Simulation-driven intelligence across the lifecycle Integrate product and production models spanning mechanics, electronics, software, and automation to design, simulate, and virtually commission systems before physical build, reducing risk, cost, and time to market. Closed-loop optimization with real-world data Capture data through sensors and IT/OT convergence, feed performance insights back into the twin, and use AI to analyze scenarios, predict behavior, and continuously improve throughput, quality, and scheduling. From predictive insight to autonomous decisions Train AI models in the virtual environment, deploy executable twins close to the asset, and enable real-time analysis and decision support that increases resilience, efficiency, and sustainability across operations. Supercharging your industry with the digital twin Discover how integrating reality and digital worlds enables faster innovation, sustainability and reducing costs. Read the whitepaper Explore the latest real world cases all Siemens & Hyundai: Digital twins in shipbuilding The world is changing faster than industries can adapt. The technologies to meet that challenge exist. Getting them into operation, at scale, is the work. Read the Hyundai case all Digital Twin Composer: Meet the metaverse Siemens unveils breakthrough industrial metaverse technology, delivering true industrial intelligence, by bringing together physical AI with the power of the most comprehensive digital twin. Learn more about the PepsiCo case all Digital twin technology for manufacturing Optimize production systems with flexible simulations that integrate lean methods, predict performance, and enable end-to-end process improvement. Learn how Siemens uses digital twin all Hymer Hymer leverages Siemens Xcelerator for the digital creation and prototyping of Venture S, transforming the process from design to production with efficient digital twins. Read the Hymer case all Ráječek Farm AI-powered digital twin technology optimizes sustainable strawberry farming by improving monitoring and harvesting efficiency, reducing manual labor and cutting costs. Read the agriculture case all Nautilus Natilus uses Siemens Xcelerator for innovative aircraft design, enhancing sustainability with reduced fuel consumption and immersive engineering solutions through augmented reality. Read the Nautilus case Introducing Digital Twin Composer Unveiled at CES 2026, our new Digital Twin Composer brings the industrial metaverse to life. See how we're empowering companies to build, test, and optimize in a virtual world before breaking ground in the real one. Explore our new product Unlock new possibilities with a digital twin See how AI-powered digital twins deliver real-world impact. The Audi case study explores virtual PLCs in automotive production, while our article shows how simulation allows companies to test scenarios and optimize performance before changes hit the shop floor. Case study all Audi is virtualizing the shop floor with virtual PLC What’s next for the automotive industry? Rainer Brehm, CEO of Siemens Factory Automation and Henning Löser, Head of Innovation at Audi, discuss the impact and benefits of industrial AI solutions. all Time travel with the digital twin Article Simulation capabilities have emerged as the key to adapting to the changes of today's environment. Companies can leverage this technology to remain competitive through managing industry challenges. Explore digital twin simulation Where digital twins meet and collaborate Leverage the industrial metaverse to seamlessly integrate technologies like cloud and edge computing, industrial AI and digital twins Bring your digital twins into the metaverse
